name: Mathura
description: Mathura, a city steeped in ancient history and divine significance, holds a paramount position in Hindu mythology as the birthplace of Lord Krishna. Situated on the banks of the sacred Yamuna River in Uttar Pradesh, India, Mathura is considered one of the seven holiest cities (Sapta Puri) in Hinduism. Its history stretches back to ancient times, having been a prominent center of trade and culture. However, it is primarily revered for its association with Krishna, the eighth avatar of Vishnu. The city witnessed Krishna's birth in a prison cell, his childhood adventures in Gokul and Vrindavan nearby, and his eventual triumph over the tyrannical King Kansa. Mathura's landscape is dotted with numerous temples dedicated to Krishna and his consort, Radha, each echoing tales of their divine love and playful exploits. The city's vibrant culture is deeply intertwined with Krishna's legends, which are celebrated through festivals like Janmashtami, Holi, and Radhashtami. Mathura serves as a powerful reminder of the eternal struggle between good and evil, the importance of devotion, and the transformative power of divine love. Pilgrims from across the world flock to Mathura to experience its spiritual aura and immerse themselves in the stories of Krishna, seeking blessings and a deeper connection with the divine. The city's rich artistic heritage, including its distinctive style of sculpture and painting, further enriches its cultural landscape, making it a timeless center of Hindu faith and tradition. The very air of Mathura resonates with the chants of 'Radhe Radhe' and the echoes of Krishna's flute, solidifying its place as a sacred destination for seekers of truth and devotion.
subtitle: Birthplace of Lord Krishna

keyMyths.title: Krishna's Birth
keyMyths.description: Lord Krishna was born in a prison cell in Mathura to Devaki and Vasudeva, as the eighth avatar of Vishnu, destined to overthrow the tyrannical King Kansa. Vasudeva secretly carried the infant Krishna across the Yamuna River to Gokul to protect him from Kansa's wrath.

keyMyths.title: Krishna's Victory over Kansa
keyMyths.description: After growing up in Gokul and Vrindavan, Krishna returned to Mathura and defeated his uncle, King Kansa, fulfilling the prophecy of his birth. This event marked the triumph of good over evil and established Krishna's divine authority.

sources.author: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
sources.description: A comprehensive commentary on the Srimad Bhagavatam, detailing the life and teachings of Krishna.
sources.text: A comprehensive commentary on the Srimad Bhagavatam, detailing the life and teachings of Krishna.
